rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BELARDE VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Albay Construction Companys Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRANDIE PITTMAN et al VS. BURNHAM LLC (FKA BURNHAM CORPORATION WHICH WILL DO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 5, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Superior Boiler Works, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 2, 3, 5 and 6, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Anthony Hale, Sr. was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Finigan's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Assuming that Mr. Helfrich's declaration is admissible, it is insufficient to demonstrate that Mr. Hale did not work with/around a Superior Boiler Works boiler. When read in conjunction with Mr. Finigan's testimony, it merely creates a triable issue of material fact. Defendant's motion did not raise the issue of asbestos content of any products or materials for which it is responsible, but even if it did, the testimony of Mr. Helfrich would create a triable issue whether the gaskets identified by Mr. Finigan to which Mr. Hale was exposed contained asbestos. Summary adjudication issue 4 (false representation) is denied as moot because a review of plaintiffs' complaint demonstrates that cause of action was not alleged against defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Finigan's declaration are overruled. Had defendant properly raised the issue of asbestos content, defendant's objections to its General Order No. 129 interrogatory responses and to the prior testimony of Robert Helfrich would be overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
